facts.object_value: DEFENSE ARGUMENT: Khanna's PTR filings list multiple Ahuja family trusts explicitly labeled "Irrev" (Irrevocable): (1) "Monte and Usha Ahuja 2010 Irrev Trust FBO" — an irrevocable trust For Benefit Of (FBO) named beneficiaries created by Ritu's parents Monte and Usha Ahuja; (2) "Ahuja [Khanna] Children Irrevocable Trust" — explicit irrevocable trust for Khanna's children; (3) "Ahuja Grandchildren's Education Trust" — typical irrevocable estate planning vehicle; (4) "Ritu Ahuja 1994 Trust" — created when Ritu was ~21, before meeting Khanna (~2003), classic irrevocable family gift trust by Monte (Transtar Industries founder, est. $500M net worth). ASSESSMENT: If these trusts are (a) irrevocable, (b) the beneficiary has no power to direct investments, (c) trustee is a third party, then under STOCK Act + Ethics in Government Act §101(f)(1)(G) these holdings remain reportable but asset-management discretion lies with trustee, softening intent-based claims. COUNTER-EVIDENCE: "RituAhuja Declaration of Trust" (also in PTR) is likely a REVOCABLE living trust — standard estate planning form; "2020 Trust FBO Khanna Children" was created in 2020 DURING Khanna's Congressional tenure, meaning Khanna had direct knowledge of trust creation and likely approved its investment composition. VULNERABILITY: cannot verify irrevocability or trustee identity from public records — requires subpoena of trust instruments. IMPACT ON THEORY 101: moderates but does not eliminate STOCK Act exposure. Partial...[truncated]

PEER BASELINE ANALYSIS: Khanna's PTR filing frequency is at the 99.29th percentile of the House of Representatives and #2 of 27 active filers on HASC. (1) House-wide: 1,962 distinct House members with at least one PTR 2013-2026. Median 1 filing, p75=2, p95=24, Khanna=114 (4.75x p95, 114x median). Top 1% of active traders in the House. (2) HASC-specific: pool of 36 known 118th/119th HASC members; 27 have any PTR filing. Khanna at 114 is #2 only to committee Chair Mike Rogers (R-AL, 151). HASC mean 29.8 filings, median 8.0, Khanna=3.8x mean, 14.25x median. (3) Only three other HASC members cross 100 filings: Rogers, Khanna, Gottheimer (107); the 4th (Smith D-WA Ranking Member) is at 96. These four represent <15% of HASC but account for 50%+ of all HASC PTR-reported activity. (4) Most HASC members file zero or one PTR (classic buy-and-hold or non-stock-holders) — the committee distribution is extremely bimodal. IMPLICATION: Defense cannot argue "every congressman trades this much" — the data shows the opposite. Khanna is a statistical outlier inside HASC and vs the House as a whole. CAVEAT: filing count is not trade count; some members bundle many trades per annual PTR while Khanna files monthly PTRs. CAVEAT 2: actual peer volume comparison requires re-extracting all 27 HASC members' 1,130+ attachments (114 done for Khanna), deferred to §1.4 audit follow-up.

Khanna family actively trades PALANTIR TECHNOLOGIES (PLTR) including PUT OPTIONS while Khanna is Ranking Member of HSAS35 (Cyber/IT/Innovation). 22 PLTR trades 2021-12-14 through 2026-03-30: (1) 6 PUT OPTION sells on 2026-03-16 (PUT/PLTR SEP 18 26 $130 and MAR 19 27 $130) = bullish bet stock stays above $130; (2) Regular PLTR buys $1,001-$15,000; (3) Two $15,001-$50,000 purchases (2025-04-23, 2026-01-29). HSAS35 has direct jurisdiction over defense tech procurement, AI/ML contracting. Khanna became Ranking Member 118th (2023), PLTR trading ACCELERATED afterward.

Committee assignment data (24,593 rows, 113th-119th, from unitedstates/congress-legislators) confirms Khanna on HSAS entire career: 115th rank 25, 116th rank 15, 117th rank 13, 118th rank 8 + HSAS35 Ranking Member, 119th rank 7 + HSAS35 + HSZS (China Competition) Ranking Member. Also: Oversight (116th-119th), Budget (115th-116th), Agriculture (117th only).

USAspending data shows PALANTIR TECHNOLOGIES INC + PALANTIR USG INC received $288M+ in federal contracts across 2023-2025. Top awards: $70M DoD (2024-06-26), $61M DoD (2025-06-26), $41M DoD (2024-12-18), $35M DoD (2025-01-27), $30M DHS (2025-09-25, 2025-04-11), $25M VA (2023-09-29, 2024-09-30), $24M DoD (2023-03-15), $20M DHS (2023-09-19), $20M HHS (2024-06-05), $19M DoD (2024-09-26), $19M DoD (2024-04-30), $19M DHS (2024-09-09), $15M DoD (2024-07-22), $13M DoD (2024-04-10), $12M DoE (2024-05-28), $11M HHS (2024-01-31, 2024-09-27), $11M State (2024-09-30, 2024-09-26). Agencies: DoD (dominant), VA, HHS, DHS, State, Energy. Khanna's HSAS35 subcommittee has oversight of DoD technology procurement — the exact pipeline these contracts flow through.

Cross-referencing PLTR PTR trade dates against Palantir federal contract awards (>$5M) reveals 79 proximity matches within 14 days, including TWO SAME-DAY matches: (1) 2025-09-19: Khanna family PURCHASES PLTR ($1,001-$15,000) on the SAME DAY Palantir receives a $19.4M DHS contract + 2 days after a $14.4M DoD contract + 4 days after a $22.5M DoD contract + 6 days before a $29.9M DHS contract; (2) 2025-08-26: Khanna family PURCHASES PLTR on the SAME DAY Palantir receives a $7M DoD contract. Additional near-misses: 2025-02-25 purchase = 1 day before $24.8M DoD contract; 2025-01-17 purchase = 2 days after $7.6M DoD contract; 2024-10-14 purchase = 6 days after $5.4M DoD contract; 2024-10-30 purchase = 5 days after $5.4M DoD contract. Note: contract award dates are public on USAspending at time of action, and Khanna's HSAS35 subcommittee has direct oversight of DoD technology procurement. The 2025-09-19 cluster is the most striking: PLTR buy on a day when $19.4M DHS + recent $22.5M DoD + upcoming $29.9M DHS all land within one week.

CHRIS ISRAEL served as US Coordinator for International IP Enforcement and Deputy Chief at the Department of Commerce (covered_position in LDA revolving door disclosure, filing_year=2025). After leaving Commerce, Israel registered as a lobbyist (lda_revolving_door.is_revolving_door=true). Israel ALSO appears as a donor to Khanna committees ($500 in FEC Schedule A). This completes the T601 revolving door chain: Commerce Dept official → registered lobbyist → Khanna campaign donor. While $500 is small, the structural relationship matters: a former Commerce official who lobbies on Commerce-related issues and donates to the congressman who formerly served as Deputy Assistant Secretary of Commerce. Additional Commerce revolving door lobbyists found (not yet cross-matched): Adam Wilczewski (Chief of Staff ITA), Kevin Gluba (Senior Advisor Commerce), Mary Cull (Special Advisor Office of Space Commerce).

Khanna cast 102 NDAA-related rollcall votes across 8 annual NDAA bills (FY2018-FY2026): HR 2810 (FY2018), HR 5515 (FY2019), HR 2500/S 1790 (FY2020), HR 6395 (FY2021), HR 4350 (FY2022), HR 2670 (FY2023), HR 8070 (FY2024), HR 3838 (FY2026). Voting pattern: Khanna votes YEA on final conference reports (bipartisan) but NAY on initial Republican-written versions and party-line rule votes. Key NDAA dates for trade timing: FY2018 passage 2017-07-14, FY2019 passage 2018-05-24, FY2020 passage 2019-07-12, FY2021 passage 2020-07-21, FY2024 passage 2024-06-14, FY2026 H.R. 3838 2025-09-10. Cross-ref: defense stock trades occurring within 14 days of these NDAA action dates would indicate informed trading around legislative events he directly participates in as HSAS member.

DIVERGENCE EVIDENCE — Khanna's PUBLIC voting record on NDAA bills (final passage / conference reports) is NEAR-UNIFORMLY AGAINST: 13 NDAA passage/conference rolls 2017-2024, NAY 12, YEA only ONCE (HR 2500 2019-07-12, the heavily-amended Smith version). Detail: HR 2810 NAY 2017-07-14 + NAY 2017-11-14; HR 5515 NAY 2018-05-24 + NAY 2018-07-26; HR 2500 AYE 2019-07-12; HR 6395 NAY 2020-07-21 + 2020-12-08 + 2020-12-28 (override); HR 4350 NAY 2021-09-23; S 1605 NAY 2021-12-07; HR 2670 NAY 2023-07-14; HR 8070 NAY 2024-06-14; HR 5009 NAY 2024-12-11. CRITICAL: F77 documents the family BUYING defense primes within +/-14 days of the SAME NDAA enactments — including 6 majors purchased 2017-11-30 (16 days AFTER the NAY conference vote, 12 days BEFORE law) and 4 majors purchased 2020-12-28 (the SAME DAY he voted NAY on the override). Family is trading on the FACT that the bill will pass while Khanna publicly votes against, maintaining progressive credentials.

Monthly cadence of Ritu Ahuja Khanna's XSP PUT-option writing (282 trades 2017-2026) shows systematic 2-6 options per month baseline across Khanna's entire House tenure. LDA §203 / LD-203 contribution reporting | Citation: Lobbying Disclosure Act of 1995, as amended by HLOGA, 2 U.S.C. §1604(d) | HOLDING: Registered lobbyists and their employing entities must file semiannual LD-203 reports disclosing: (1) all FECA contributions ≥$200 to federal candidates/leadership PACs/party committees; (2) all payments for events honoring covered officials; (3) all contributions to entities named for or controlled by covered officials. Civil penalties up to $200,000; criminal penalties up to 5 years imprisonment for knowing-and-corrupt violations (§1606(b)). | KEY QUOTE: "Each registrant under this Act, and each employee of such registrant who is listed or required to be listed as a lobbyist … shall file … a report — (1) containing the name of the employee or registrant; (2) containing the name of each Federal candidate or officeholder … to whom, and the date and amount of, any contribution of $200 or more made by the registrant or employee." 2 U.S.C. §1604(d)(1). | APPLICABILITY: T501 (lobbying-pressure-map) core disclosure regime. Pairs with LDA §4 registration filings to establish lobbyist-to-Member contribution flows. Cross-cite for T601 revolving-door when ex-staff lobbyists continue bundling to their former principals.

HLOGA §305 / House Rule XXI(9) (earmark disclosure) | Citation: Honest Leadership and Open Government Act of 2007, P.L. 110-81 §404; House Rule XXI clause 9 | HOLDING: No bill/joint-resolution/conference report may be considered unless a list identifying each congressional earmark, limited tax benefit, or limited tariff benefit is printed in the bill report together with the name of the requesting Member. Each requesting Member must provide a written statement certifying that the Member has no financial interest (other than constituent-service) in the earmark. Violations enforced by the Committee on Ethics. | KEY QUOTE: "It shall not be in order to consider … a bill or joint resolution … or a conference report thereon … unless the report of the Committee … or joint explanatory statement … includes a list of congressional earmarks, limited tax benefits, and limited tariff benefits … together with the name of any Member, Delegate, Resident Commissioner, or Senator who submitted a request to the committee of jurisdiction for each respective item." House Rule XXI(9)(a). | APPLICABILITY: T701 (district-enrichment) — when a Member-directed federal spending item flows to a donor entity, the §305 written-statement regime is the disclosure obligation to audit. Absence of a filed statement, or a false 'no financial interest' certification, is a Rule XXI(9) violation and OCE-reviewable.
